untranslatable_reason: This signal requires option-specific inputs (theoretical option price, market option price, implied volatility) that are not available in the allowed indicator set. The rule is designed for options traders comparing derivatives pricing vs market quotes, which requires volatility surface data and option pricing models (Black-Scholes inputs). None of the allowed indicators (price, moving averages, RSI, ATR, Bollinger, MACD, ADX, Stochastic, Donchian, Pivots, volume ratios, VIX) can directly measure theoretical vs market option price divergence or IV convergence. This signal cannot be mechanically translated into equity/futures technical indicator rules.
translation_notes: NATENBERG_DRY_33_0 is a volatility-arbitrage/option-pricing signal requiring derivatives analytics. It focuses on investigating IV when option prices diverge from theory after verifying inputs (underlying, rates). The HIGH_VOL and TRENDING regimes are appropriate target contexts. Recommend implementing as a manual rule set requiring live option chain analysis and volatility modeling rather than a pure technical indicator backtest.
